description_long: This object is a large, rectangular stone block with a stepped profile, featuring a thick, upright back section and a lower, protruding front ledge. The block is composed of solid, gray stone with a matte, slightly rough surface texture, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance. The front face is divided into three segments at the base, creating two recessed notches that may serve as footings or to interlock with other structures. The sides reveal the stepped design, with the back section rising vertically and the front ledge extending outward. The top surface is flat and broad, suitable for supporting weight or serving as a seating area. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements, and the block appears to be in good condition with no significant weathering.
description: A large, gray stone block with a stepped profile, flat top, and recessed notches at the base, featuring a matte, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stepped profile, flat top, and three recessed notches at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thick, upright back section and the lower, protruding front ledge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, flat surface with no visible features or notches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the stepped profile and showing the depth of the block.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or public park seating area
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely freestanding or placed as a single barrier or seat
